title: ALerts AI Playbook usecases.json
description: The data contract between the curator agent (write, publish-usecase) and the static Playbook reader (read). Sibling of articles.schema.json.

id.description: Stable unique slug; used in the usecase.html?id= URL, e.g. reddit-recursive-prompting-2026-07-10.

tools.description: Tool names the technique applies to; drives the tool filter + sanctioned toggle.

category.description: One of the four Playbook categories.

whatItImproves.description: 1-2 sentences: the before/after — time saved, quality gained, step removed.

sourcePlatform.description: Which Playbook source the entry came from.

author.description: Handle/name credit for the original poster (optional).

difficulty.description: Self-select hint for a mixed-skill audience (optional).

curatorVerified.description: Set at the confirmation gate; default false.

publishedDate.description: Original publication date of the source (YYYY-MM-DD).

addedDate.description: When the curator published it via the agent (YYYY-MM-DD); defaults repo-side to publish day.

audience.description: Which i2e roles genuinely benefit from the technique: developers (Developers/Coders), qa (QA), ba-pc (BAs & Project Coordinators), pm (Project Managers), non-technical (Non-technical users). Optional.

whyRelevant.description: Answers 'Why is this relevant to me?' for an i2e reader.

dailyImpact.description: Answers 'How will this help in my daily job?'

practicalBenefit.description: Answers 'What practical benefit does it provide?'

relevance.description: Reader-facing relevance framing for i2e employees; optional, translates the technique into the reader's workday.
